summary: Remove libmongoc hacks
description:
pongo.c contains aweful hacks to break into the libmongoc private API.
These things need to be fixed in libmongoc (and I believe already are).

The hacks need to be removed and the code fixed to use the exported API

summary: Use ZendMM for libbson
description:
libBSON has support for swapping out the MM now.
We should use the Zend MM so we can report correct statistics and obey memory_limits

summary: Setup coverity
description:
Coverity Scan is now open and easily added for open source projects.

It allows us to do up to 3 scans a day, or maximum of 7 scans per 7days.

summary: Add ReadPreferences and WriteConcern support
Fix invalid free()
description:
Our bson_free() wrapper needs to make sure we don't free \0
The PHP allocator doesn't check it

summary: Memory leak on namespace access
description:
phongo_split_namespace() duplicates the dbname and collection names.
These need to be properly free()d

summary: PHP thinks we are leaking streams
description:
When compiling PHP in debug mode (ZEND_DEBUG) it will errournously warn about leaking userland exposed stream resources.

We need to tell the engine specifically that the resource is supposed to be exposed

summary: Debug output written to stderr
description:
The phongo.debug_log is set to stderr by default.

This causes a flood of unhelpful output by default.

summary: Implement WriteConcerns
description: WriteConcerns are currently ignored :)

summary: Add support for "Command cursors"
$batch->update() fails when replacing entire document
Rename count methods in WriteResults
Document install instructions from vcs
description:
Currently, some tricks (git submodule init && git submodule update && make patch) need to be executed. If you don't know this, you can't compile the extension.

In order to make it easy for our (upcoming) users to install this, libmongoc and libbson should be bundled - and their build systems integrated into the PHP build system.

summary: Remove ssl=prefer support
Embed phongo-crud tests in phongo
description:
Before releasing phongo we need to ensure that all the phongo-crud tests pass to.

Running the phongo-crud tests will also boost our coverage of phongo.

Killing multiple birds with one stone: embed phongo-crud tests in phongo.

summary: MacOSX build failure
description:
librt is hardcoded in config.m4 and causes linking errors on MacOSX.

We already conditionally check for earlier, so no need to hardcode it.

summary: DBRef isn't a real BSON type
description:
BSON\DBRef is not actually a real bson type.
Its a convention, and therefore should not be part of phongo - but phongo libraries, maybe?

summary: Cursor->getId() vs Cursor->getCursorId()
description:
These seem to be the same method - where one is implemented not the other.

Cursor->getId() should be sufficient.

summary: Add MongoDB\Cursor->kill()
description: Since we drill into the mongoc API willynilly now, we can access the cursor on client so we can kill it.

summary: connectiontimeoutms not supported
description: The connecttimeoutms doesn't do anything yet

summary: Ensure that connections a persisted per distinct MongoClient arguments
description:
The 1.x driver requires replica set names to be unique (PHP-729) and also redundantly connects to non-canonical seed list hosts on subsequent requests (PHP-1211). There are also concerns with persistent, authenticated connections being shared by multiple MongoClient instances.

Ideally, persistent connections should be hashed by a unique set of MongoClient arguments, including the URI and relevant connection options (e.g. SSL, auth, timeouts, but not read preferences or default write concern). With this strategy, the driver might still connect and discard connections to non-canonical seed list hosts, but subsequent requests would immediately find the persistent connections to utilize based on the MongoClient argument hash. Additionally, there would be no concern of connections being shared between MongoClient instances, nor would there be any conflict with duplicate replica set names.

summary: Throw AuthenticationException on auth failure
description: We should throw MongoDB\AuthenticationException (extending MongoDB\ConnectionException) when we detect authentication failure.

summary: WriteConcernError is just one error, not multiple
description:
We current have WriteResult->getWriteConcernErrors()

Besides never actually being populated due to mongoc bug, it can only ever hold one error.

See https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server_write_commands.rst#write-concern-failure

summary: Use MongoDB\Driver namespace for phongo
description:
The core driver should be in its own namespace.
This avoids the problem of not knowing if a class is a userland or core driver component.

The driver will use MongoDB\Driver
while the core tools can use MongoDB

summary: Simplify bson deserialization code
Create PHP interface for BSON deserialization
BSON\toArray should support a classmap to unserialize as
Option for retrieving documents as object rather then arrays
description: We need an option, similar to $assoc=bool in json_decode() (http://php.net/json_decode) to be able to give the user the chance of decoding the documents as objects or an array

summary: Use BSON\Serialize::bsonSerialize() during serialization
Wrong sizeof modifying INI in server.c
Wrong sizeof in INI entry
Pointer to out-of-scope variable
Buffer not null terminated
Unchecked return
Setting debug output to STDERR on the manager writes to resource file
var_dump()ing Query and Command should show the query/command being built
var_dump()ing WriteResults should give meaningful results
description: Should include things like the writeConcern results and write info.

summary: var_dump()ing Cursor should be meaningful
MongoDB\Driver\Query::FLAG_OPLOG_REPLY is misspelled
description:
When implementing the HHVM prototype, I noticed that the FLAG_OPLOG_REPLY constant is misspelled. It should be FLAG_OPLOG_REPLAY as that's how it's defined at http://docs.mongodb.org/meta-driver/latest/legacy/mongodb-wire-protocol/#op-query

The C driver also has this wrong, and I've filed issue CDRIVER-505 for this.

summary: Skip auth tests when servers configurations not available
description:
Currently our skipif strategy only does bare basic test, e.g. if we have the main mongod to talk to.

Tests should verify that the mongod server they need to talk to is available before running its tests.

summary: Typo in example
ReadPreferences swapped
description:
When specifying ReadPreference::RP_PRIMARY_PREFERRED, we actually send secondary.
When specifying ReadPreference::RP_SECONDARY, we actually send primaryPreferred

summary: MO changes in X509 auth
description:
MO has radically changed how to launch X509 servers and create initial users.
This breaks our testsuite.

Previously we were using a custom patch that no longer applies as a "final" version of this issue has now been merged into master.

summary: Document the ODS
description:
0.1.4 includes a Object Document Serializer using the BSON\Persistable interface.

This allows users to serialize classes to documents, and the documents back to those original classes

summary: Throw specific exceptions for maxTimeMS timeouts (commands and queries)
description:
In the 1.x driver, MongoExecutionTimeoutException is only thrown for maxTimeMS timeouts during queries. Command helpers may only throw MongoResultException after inspecting the command result's {{"ok"}} field. Ideally, we'd have specific exceptions for their maxTimeMS timeouts as well.

This is tricky in 1.x, since MongoExecutionTimeoutException is not an instance of MongoResultException, which users currently know to expect. Introducing a new exception in 1.x could be a BC break, so this is probably best considered for the next major version of the driver.

Note: I'm not counting MongoCursorTimeoutException here, which is thrown by everything for client-side socket timeouts.

summary: Throw MongoDB\Driver\ConnectionTimeoutException
description:
Should be thrown when we hit socketimeoutms.
Note that this value cannot be overridden. It defaults to 1000L * 60L * 5L and can only be set through the Manager URI (?sockettimeoutms=xxx)

summary: s/Batch/Bulk/g
description:
I'm not sure how this came to be (maybe stubbern Icelandic person), but all our batch/bulk things are called "Batch" ($batch, WriteBatch, ->executeWriteBatch, ...).

All other drivers, and specs, refer to this as Bulk.

summary: Serializing and unserializing internal classes causes segfault
Throw WriteException & BulkWriteException on write failure
description:
Currently it is fairly complicated to check if a write succeeded or not.
We don't throw an exception on writeerror nor writeconcernerror - but we do on connection and other related errors.

Furthermore, the user has to check both Result->getWriteErrors() and Result->getWriteConcernError() to see if there was an error.
This doesn't make whole lot of sense :/


We should throw MongoDB\Driver\BulkWriteException on bulkWrite failure, which contains ->getWriteResult() which returns the original WriteResult object.
The WriteResult object then has ->getWriteErrors() and ->getWriteConcernError().

For execute[Insert|Update|Delete]() we unwrap the BulkWriteException and throw DuplicateKeyException, WriteConcernException or generic WriteException on failure.

summary: HIPPO can't throw InvalidArgumentException on argument errors
description:
Current vast amounts of methods change the default PHP error handling that throws a warning to throwing exceptions. This should not be done because:

 - PHP's general error handling for nearly any function or method, albeit in core or in an extension doesn't do this
 - It is incompatible with HHVM's error handling. In HHVM, the parameter parsing happens before the (native) C-function is entered. This makes it impossible to convert warnings to exceptions, *even if we wanted to*.

summary: SSL Doesn't work in PHP5.6
Don't set write concern on mongoc_bulk_operation_t unnecessarily
description: Calling {{mongoc_bulk_operation_set_write_concern(NULL)}} (i.e. when we know no write concern zval was received by a write method) results in libmongoc overwriting the existing write concern with a new object. Ideally, we should inherit the client's write concern.

summary: WriteConcern debug output ignores all fields for w default
description:
When {{w}} is {{-2}} (libmongoc's default), our debug handler hides rendering of other fields in the debug output. This is especially problematic because explicitly providing {{w=1}} to libmongoc (e.g. in URI options) results in -2 being stored. I assume the current behavior was added by thinking {{MONGOC_WRITE_CONCERN_W_DEFAULT}} meant the entire write concern was defaulted (not just {{w}}).

It would be more straightforward if we always print all fields. Additionally, I think we can still render {{w}} as {{"majority"}} alongside our synthetic {{wmajority}} boolean field, since that is the actual value being passed in {{w}}. Users can still rely on our boolean to avoid a string comparison check.

This is what IteratorIterator in PHP are meant for anyway, there is no need for us to specifically wrap our iterator with a user provided class.
Besides, this would complicate our internals needing to call into userland iterator rather then our precompiled iterators.

summary: Result::toArray() should proxy iterator_to_array($this)
description:
This was previously discussed in PHPC-157 when consolidating the command and query result classes. Accessing the singular result document of a command should require the user to do {{$result->toArray()[0]}}.

Currently, this method converts {{firstBatch}} from BSON to a zval (ignoring any custom type map in the process), which leads to inconsistent behavior between results for queries, commands that return a cursor, and commands that do not return a cursor.

summary: Result::toArray() ignores type map configuration
description: {{Result::setTypeMap()}} appears to be completely ignored by {{Result::toArray()}}. It's possible that fixing PHPC-203 will implicitly resolve this issue, but we should track it as its own bug regardless.

Support global stream context
description: The 1.x driver currently calls {{php_stream_context_from_zval()}} with {{PHP_FILE_NO_DEFAULT_CONTEXT}} to disable inheritance of the global stream context (if there is one). We should add support for the global stream context.

summary: Use explicit SSL options rather then stream context
description:
Stream contexts are hip and cool, but cumbersome.
The user provided stream context also is just one context, while we connect to many servers.
Any further changes by the user of the context will not apply to all the streams.

This makes it a bit confusing.

Furthermore, it adds several lines of "bootstrapping" needed to get SSL working.
We can also simply get an array and make our own contexts - providing consistent verification behaviour across PHP versions.

See tests/standalone/bug0166.phpt

summary: Implement version check
description:
Userland needs a way to know the server version so it can do the poor mans feature discovery.

This in particular is required for returning aggregate cursors in 2.6, where as the switch fails on 2.4

summary: Do not allow BSON arrays to be hydrated as PHP classes
description:
When {{php_phongo_bson_visit_document()}} and {{php_phongo_bson_visit_array()}} iterate on the keys within objects and arrays, respectively, the class name picked up by {{php_phongo_bson_visit_binary()}} is used for hydration and the unserialize method (i.e. {{BSON_UNSERIALIZE_FUNC_NAME}}) is invoked directly. From what I can tell, this code doesn't appear to check if the class itself implements the required interface.

It also means that BSON arrays might get hydrated as classes *iff* they contain an appropriate binary data element. Unlike objects, I don't think we should rely on arrays containing elements, given that update operators are prone to removing elements from an array (see: [{{$push}} with {{$slice}}|http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/tutorial/limit-number-of-elements-in-updated-array/]). Also, this would mean that size checks done on the array during queries or in other drivers would differ from the size of the hydrated array in the PHP driver.

If the user would like a BSON array to be hydrated as a PHP class, that is best done in the parent document classe that contains the array/collection field.

summary: php_phongo_bson_visit_document() should check class interface before unserializing
description:
As first mentioned in PHPC-209:

bq. When {{php_phongo_bson_visit_document()}} and {{php_phongo_bson_visit_array()}} iterate on the keys within objects and arrays, respectively, the class name picked up by {{php_phongo_bson_visit_binary()}} is used for hydration and the unserialize method (i.e. BSON_UNSERIALIZE_FUNC_NAME) is invoked directly. From what I can tell, this code doesn't appear to check if the class itself implements the required interface.

summary: Consolidate Result and Cursor into a single, Traversable Cursor class
description:
Here's the deal:

 * The current Cursor class and its Iterator implementation can be deleted
 * Copy over the {{isDead()}} implementation over to Result
 * Result can simply be Traversable
 ** IteratorAggregate was only used because we were going to support custom iterator classes (but no longer as of PHPC-214)
 ** Internal classes can implement Travserable by having a {{get_iterator()}} handler. Down the line, a mongofill or userland clone of Phongo can simply implement Iterator or IteratorAggregate as needed to still work with PHPLIB.
 * Result can now be renamed to Cursor (we still want that name)

The constructors of value objects that represent internal structures should be private
description:
As discussed in our last meeting, the constructors of the following classes should be marked as private (and the classes as final):

 - MongoDB/Driver/CursorID
 - MongoDB/Driver/Cursor
 - MongoDB/Driver/Server (already done, but also has a ctor implementation)
 - MongoDB/Driver/WriteConcernError (currently has no ctor at all)
 - MongoDB/Driver/WriteError (currently has no ctor at all)
 - MongoDB/Driver/WriteResult (currently has no ctor at all)

summary: Remove unused RINIT and RSHUTDOWN handlers
description: There is no need to provide these since we aren't about to use them.

summary: Cannot iterate beyond command cursor's first batch
Skipping/default value for "*ms" URI paremeters inconsistent
empty array should be serialized as array
Allow ->setTypeMap() to set 'array' and 'stdclass'
description:
Currently you can only use object instanceof BSON\Unserializable.

We need to be able to set to standard 'array's too, so we can get bson documents as PHP arrays.

And we need to allow passing 'stdclass' as that is the default value for bson documents.

summary: executeUpdate() converts objects to arrays
Cannot use object of type Person as array
refcount failure in cursor
Manager->executeUpdate() option is supposed to be 'multi'
description:
For some reason the flag for updating multiple documents is incorrectly named.

It should be called multi: <bool>
See: http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/reference/command/update/#dbcmd.update

summary: Can't build on RHEL-6 (old autoconf)
description:
As mongodb still have PHP 5.3 support, I think it make sense to be able to build on some old distro.

+ /usr/bin/phpize
Configuring for:
PHP Api Version:         20100412
Zend Module Api No:      20100525
Zend Extension Api No:   220100525
configure:6907: error: possibly undefined macro: AS_CASE
      If this token and others are legitimate, please use m4_pattern_allow.
      See the Autoconf documentation.
configure:6912: error: possibly undefined macro: AS_EXECUTABLE_P
configure:6912: error: possibly undefined macro: AS_IF
configure:6913: error: possibly undefined macro: AC_CHECK_PROGS

summary: Allow to build with system libbson
description: See https://github.com/10gen-labs/mongo-php-driver-prototype/pull/38

summary: MongoDB/WriteResult.c's function table refers to Server, __construct and Manager, __wakeUp
Consistent tree for tests
description:
After installation (via pecl install) some file are in "docdir", some in "testdir"

Proposal: 

sed -e '/name="scripts/s/role="doc"/role="src"/' \
    -e '/Vagrantfile/s/role="doc"/role="src"/' \
    -i package.xml

summary: Add MongoDB\Driver\UnexpectedValueException
description: When we encounter unexpected values, such as during bson serialization, we should throw MongoDB\Driver\UnexpectedValueException that extends spl UnexpectedValueException, implementing MongoDB\Driver\Exception

summary: BSON encoding unsupoprted types (Resource) should fail
_id generated on embedded document
Allow "object" as an alias of "stdClass" for setTypeMap()
Remove composer dependencies for tests
Several test fail because of missing enableTestCommands
description:
Several tests are failing because mongod isn't running with testCommandsEnabled as a --setParameter.

We should check that mongod is running with the needed options, or else skip the test.

summary: mongodb.debug_log improvements -> mongodb.debug
description:
It'd be nice if the debug log (mongodb.debug_log) could have microsecond precision.

I'm wondering what other things it should include to make it a one-stop-gap when requesting debug logs.

- Rename to mongodb.debug
- driver name (phongo/hippo)
- driver version
- mongoc and libbson versions
- connection string
- connection options


The mongodb.debug must be a directory (or stderr/stdout), not a single filename, otherwise we'll get very mangled and confusing logs when multiple processes append to the same log.

{noformat}
mongodb.debug=off/0/""
    Disables debugging, Default value is ""
mongodb.debug=on/1
    Creates new unique debug log in the system temp directory (typically /tmp) with a filename prefix PHONGO-<timestamp>
mongodb.debug=/path/to/folder
    Creates new unique debug log in the provided directory with a filename prefix PHONGO-<timestamp>
{noformat}

summary: WriteResult::getModifiedCount() should return null for legacy writes
description: We need to check libmongoc's {{mongoc_write_result_t.omit_nModified}} property and conditionally return {{null}}. This will at least allow users to discern zero from an unset value.

summary: zval_to_bson() ignores BSON\Serializable interface
description: The logic in {{zval_to_bson()}} differs from {{object_to_bson()}}, in that it ignores objects implementing {{BSON\Serializable}} and only processes classes that implement the more-specific {{BSON\Peristable}} interface.

summary: object_to_bson() should throw exception if bsonSerialize() returns non-array
WriteConcern debug handler should denote unset journal/fsync options
description: Currently, the debug handler always displays {{journal}} and {{fsync}} as booleans. If they are not set (support forthcoming in CDRIVER-580), we should print them as {{null}.

summary: WriteConcern should always set journal/fsync boolean args
description:
Currently, WriteResult only sets journal and fsync if the constructor arguments are {{true}}. As of CDRIVER-580, libmongoc differentiates between set (either {{true}} or {{false}}) or unset values. We would also like to do that in the debug handler (PHPC-179).

The constructor should always set the boolean value (if passed) on the underlying {{mongoc_write_concern_t}} struct.

summary: Throw UnexpectedValueException for invalid bsonSerialize() return value
Fix build
prep-release.php may generate empty <notes> in package.xml
description: Noticed while package 0.6.1, since there were no PHPC or CID issues in the changelog since 0.6.0. Empty notes means that PECL's package build will fail, which requires manually editing it and re-running the script.

summary: pecl package reported as 'devel' not 'alpha'
description:
https://pecl.php.net/package/mongodb/0.6.1

That should say 'alpha' not 'devel'

summary: phongo.debug created for empty values
description: Empty mongodb.debug should be treated as "false"

summary: Weird error messages when connecting to non-mongodb node
description:
{noformat}
php -n -c. -r '$m = new MongoDB\Driver\Manager("mongodb://127.0.0.1"); $m->executeQuery("foo.bar", new MongoDB\Driver\Query([]));'

Notice: MongoDB\Driver\Manager::executeQuery(): send of 4 bytes failed with errno=111 Connection refused in Command line code on line 1

Notice: MongoDB\Driver\Manager::executeQuery(): send of 4 bytes failed with errno=32 Broken pipe in Command line code on line 1

Notice: MongoDB\Driver\Manager::executeQuery(): send of 4 bytes failed with errno=32 Broken pipe in Command line code on line 1

Notice: MongoDB\Driver\Manager::executeQuery(): send of 4 bytes failed with errno=32 Broken pipe in Command line code on line 1

Notice: MongoDB\Driver\Manager::executeQuery(): send of 11 bytes failed with errno=32 Broken pipe in Command line code on line 1

Notice: MongoDB\Driver\Manager::executeQuery(): send of 4 bytes failed with errno=32 Broken pipe in Command line code on line 1

Notice: MongoDB\Driver\Manager::executeQuery(): send of 4 bytes failed with errno=32 Broken pipe in Command line code on line 1

Notice: MongoDB\Driver\Manager::executeQuery(): send of 19 bytes failed with errno=32 Broken pipe in Command line code on line 1
^C
php -n -c. -r   7.12s user 0.48s system 100% cpu 7.594 total

summary: Annoying compiler warning
Coalesce small writes
description:
Currently we just iterate over the vector and call php_stream_write().
We wind up with very fragmented write, which is not good.

summary: Do not expose users to libmongoc's cryptic connection timeout message
Ensure Query always serializes $query as a BSON object
var_dump()ing Manager should show debug info
description: When var_dump()ing the Manager we should enumerate all the server info we have

summary: Missing debug info for Server object
description: var_dump()ing a Server object should show info about the server

summary: Allow to build with system libmongoc
description: Per packaging Guidelines for downstream distrobution (Fedora and other distro)

summary: Failed getmore should throw exception
description: When a getmore fails, we should throw exception.

summary: Move BSON types to MongoDB\BSON namespace
description:
All BSON types should be under the "MongoDB\" vendor prefix, and not in a top-level namespace of their own. 

Meaning, that:

BSON\Serializable should be MongoDB\BSON\Serializable
etc.

summary: The check for system mongoc checks system bson, not mongoc
description:
The configure check checking if we are using the system or bundled libmongoc accidentally uses the bson results.

We should also include the mongoc system/bundled in the output.

summary: Wrong bugtracker URL in configure output
Ensure "sort" Query option is serialized as BSON object
description:
{{phongo_query_init()}} does {{convert_to_array_ex(&sort);}} before setting the "sort" option on the query document, which means that sorting on field "0" could result in the sort document serializing as a BSON array instead of an object.

This isn't a problem for the filter, since it's always run through {{convert_to_object(filter);}}.

summary: Check for sasl_client_done when generating mongoc-config.h
description:
mongoc added check for MONGOC_HAVE_SASL_CLIENT_DONE in their config.h

https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/238/files#diff-3b8a9191f0ea1c2e11de51012edc7bd2L41

summary: Bulk delete and updates result in one roundtrip per document
bson_to_zval() leaks when buffer contains multiple documents
Cannot connect to healthy Replica Set if seedlist only contains a secondary
Failed getmore halts for 30seconds
Top level documents should be deserialized as stdClass by default
description:
Hey - as discussed at the 2015-06-15 meeting:

| There was inconsistency issue with Hippo vs. Phongo regarding what type (stdClass or array) should be the top level type. We decided to made it consistent as what we already did for embedded documents, and make every document come back as a stdClass object. Type map should be changed to return top-level documents as stdClass.

summary: Injected __pclass should override a __pclass key in bsonSerialize() return value
fromJSON() leaks when JSON contains array or object fields
Allow bsonSerialize() to return a stdClass
Allow non-root Serializable to encode as BSON array
description:
Per the [serialization spec|https://github.com/10gen-labs/mongo-hhvm-driver-prototype/blob/master/serialization.rst]:

 * Root documents must be serialized as a BSON document.
 * MongoDB\BSON\Persistable objects must be serialized as a BSON document.
 * *If bsonSerialize returns a packed array, serialize as a BSON array.*
 * If bsonSerialize returns a non-packed array or stdClass, serialize as a BSON document.
 * If bsonSerialize did not return an array or stdClass, throw an MongoDB\Driver\Exception\UnexpectedValueException exception.

summary: bson_to_zval() should throw exceptions instead of warnings
description:
We currently trigger warnings for the following:

 * "Could not read document from reader"
 * "Could not initialize BSON iterator"
 * "Reading document did not exhaust input buffer"

Aside from {{src/contrib/php-ssl.c}}, these are the only errors/warnings emitted by the extension. We should change these to exceptions.

Additionally, this would resolve some test output differences with HHVM (see: [PR #66|https://github.com/10gen-labs/mongo-php-driver-prototype/pull/66]).

summary: bson_to_zval() leaks when buffer contains no documents
Implicit declaration of mongoc_uri_parse_host()
"invalid namespace" exception should report the namespace
Support read preference and write concern in Manager options array
description:
The current URI and option parsing logic relies on the {{mongoc_uri_option_is_TYPE()}} functions, none of which return {{true}} for {{"w"}} (since it supports multiple types).

Additionally, both the read preference and write concern structures are created once when the URI is parsed. Those structures are then set on the client. Changing the URI options for read preferences or write concerns does not update the compiled structures, which means they will need to be processed manually (i.e. get the client's structure, apply new options, and set the changed structure back on the client).

summary: Server::executeBulkWrite() should take a write concern
description: {{Server::executeBulkWrite()}} currently takes no write concern and inherits from the client object. Should it take an optional write concern like its Manager counterpart? In PHPLIB, we need to select the primary manually for APIs that depend on wire version checking, and I'm thinking of a case where legacy index creation would want to ensure we only use w:1 on the insertion.

summary: Windows build failure
zend_throw_exception() return value changes
description:
zend_throw_exception() no longer returns the zval, only the inner zend_object.

We only use it in one place, to add additional property to the exception.
A workaround would be to access EG(exception) (which also, isn't a zval anymore but zend_object) and add the property to that object.

summary: Move MongoDB\Manager->__construct work to _init method
Split object structures and retrieval
description:
In PHP7 custom object fields need to be declared first in the structures as apposed to being last in PHP5.

This also means casting zend_object is no longer possible and rather then calling zend_object_store_get_object() and cast the result, we need to do a quick XtOffsetOf dance.

summary: return_value_ptr and return_value_used removed in PHP 7
description:
return_value_ptr and return_value_used are removed in PHP 7.

We don't use return_value_ptr (other then to cast it to (void) to avoid unused-variable warnign), but return_value_used to do use in couple of cases to skip bson encoding/decoding when we know it serves no purpose.

The return_value_used should be hardcoded to 1 in PHP 7 as there is nothing similar to it anymore.

For the unused-argument warning, we can create a version macro for it.

summary: Accessing resources changes
description: zend_list_find() is removed in PHP7.

summary: Stop testing 5.3 on travis
description:
Here we go. I broke 5.3 build, which we don't support anyway (see https://github.com/10gen-labs/mongo-php-driver-prototype/commit/12742f050f3fb32e9958980de46f20ccb75eac4c).
We should stop testing it on travis

summary: TSRMLS_FETCH_FROM_CTX() not applicable in PHP 7
description:
In PHP 7, the entire TSRMLS macro love has been dropped.
In the rare event TSRMLS is needed, the old macros are not kosher, and have more or less all been defined as empty (for compatabilitiy).

However, TSRMLS_FETCH_FROM_CTX() still does assign tsrmls, but as we don't use it in PHP 7, it wind up unused and the compiler complains hiding more valuable warnings.

summary: zend_string* vs char* PHPAPI changes
description:
Many functions in the PHP API that used to take/return a char* (and length) now expect/return zend_string instead.

It'll become very messy very fast to work around this inline.
I think a phongo_char macro defining zend_string/char might work, with couple of helpers like phongo_char_free() and phongo_str() macros.

summary: MongoDB\Driver\Cursor var_dump() weird
description:
The MongoDB\Driver\Cursor var_dump() format looks weird.

All content is stuffed into a top-level "cursor" key... With only one sibling, server_id.
No other object do that I believe. BulkWrite for example doesn't have a top-level "bulkwrite" key or anything.

This also looks very wrong from the code perspective, where the returned array only has 2 elements, but we specifically initialized an array of size 4 with array_init_size().

Should the contents of the "cursor" key be moved one-level up?

summary: convert_to_*() changes
description:
As stated by PHPC-373, the use of zval*s in php_phongo.c is problematic due to huge amount of PHP API changes (no longer operates on pointers to pointers).

convert_to_*ex() for example takes a zval*, not zval**.

The places where we are doing these casts are mostly when we read things from an array and populating correct bson types.

It looks like most of these could have already been zval_to_bson() already and plucked out from the bson_t.

summary: Iterator API changes
description:
The iterators have changed a bit.
Most notably

- zend_object_iterator.data is now a zval
- iterator_get_current_data() now returns the current element

summary: Forward port Z_PHPDATE_P() to get php_date_obj from ext/date
add_assoc_string_ex() no longer has duplicate param
php_stream's no longer have resource ids
EG(exception) is zend_object, not zval, in PHP 7
Manager::getServers() should omit unknown servers
description:
When {{Manager::getServers()}} is called before cluster discovery (e.g. immediately after construction), it returns Server instances with missing information. We should filter out these servers so that only known servers are returned.

Note: previously, this ticket requested a change to ensure that at least one discovery round has been performed. We ultimately decided against that, as it makes {{getServers()}} too similar to {{selectServer()}}.

summary: PHP 7: MAKE_STD_ZVAL is a gonner
create_object changes
description: Allocating a object has changed since properties or inlined now

summary: dtor on bson_to_zval() failure
description: When bson_to_zval() fails, should the caller really need to zval_ptr_dtor() ? That seems a bit.. weird.

summary: PHONGO_TODO_SSL
description:
I've ifdef'd out some of the stream/ssl code under PHONGO_TODO_SSL

It needs porting to PHP7.

summary: PHONGO_TODO_INI
description: If ifdef'd out the INI registration and updating/altering under PHONGO_TODO_INI.

summary: PHONGO_TODO_STREAM
description: I've ifdef'd out some of the stream context code under PHONGO_TODO_STREAM, it might only need the zend_hash_find() API change though

summary: PHP 7: zend_read_property prototype is different in PHP 7
description:
zend_read_property now includes a zval for any error code returns. 

Commit for this change:
https://github.com/pboyd04/mongo-php-driver-prototype/commit/ebb827697101dbc132e2ddb245b925a50423468f

summary: PHP 7: PHP 7 changes the way INI entries are implemented
description:
The PHP 7 code base uses a new set of macros instead of relying on a static structure definition. 

Here is commit to change to using the new STD_PHP_INI_ENTRY macro:
https://github.com/pboyd04/mongo-php-driver-prototype/commit/917d45eaf83b54db2d00b4d39353c3aa39e957ec

summary: PHP 7: nApplyCount changes in HashTables
description:
PHP 7 has now added macros to access or modify apply count. I've added these macros to PHP 5 for compatibility purposes.

https://github.com/pboyd04/mongo-php-driver-prototype/commit/4211837e139369960ac8c835f138341b61350c1c

summary: dup argument in most string macros removed
description:
RETURN_STRINGL()
RETVAL_STRING()
add_*_string()

These no longer take a final "duplicate" argument.

summary: zpp 's' expects size_t
zval_used_for_init no longer applicable
Handle IS_TRUE and IS_FALSE in BSON
Hash API off-by-one changes
zval changes; zval pointer becomes zval; zval** -> zval*
description:
See https://wiki.php.net/phpng-upgrading#zval

More or less all zval* cases should be replaced with zval, and zval** with zval*.

summary: Merge Release notes
description:
At this point we're starting to flood the root folder with RELEASE-x.y.z files.

We should merge the 0.y.z notes into RELEASE-0 and start a RELEASE-1.0 file and then RELEASE-1.1 file.

summary: Encoding a DateTime instance produces no BSON
Return value objects from Manager::getWriteConcern() and getReadPreference()
description: Currently, these methods return arrays in the form of each value object's debug output. We should instead return the actual value objects, so the return values may be used with methods expecting a WriteConcern or ReadPreference.

summary: Implement getters for ReadPreference and WriteConcern properties
description: Getters may be useful so that users can inspect these objects without resorting to the debug handlers.

summary: WriteConcern constructor should accept only integer and strings for $w, without casting
WriteConcern constructor should throw for invalid $w and $wtimeout values
description:
{{mongoc_write_concern_set_w()}} requires the argument to be >= -3 or it is a NOP. We should raise an exception if {{$w}} is an integer < -3.

Additionally, should we raise an exception if the {{$wtimeout}} argument is < 0. Currently, libmongoc doesn't check for this and allows a negative timeout to be set, which may be an oversight. Per the [documentation|http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/reference/write-concern/#wtimeout], {{wtimeout}} should be >= 0.

summary: Application freezes for a minute when providing invalid hosts
Segfault when using Manager through singleton class
Query constructor may corrupt incoming options

summary: BSON encoding should ignore protected/private properties
mongoc 1.2.0-rc0[-dev] changes
description:
mongoc-1.2.0-rc0[-dev] has couple of fixes that affect phongo.

#1: The server_id can no longer be overwritten to -1 on a cursor.

This means phongo has to check if it has server_id/hint before overwriting the cursor->hint

#2: queries to standalone servers no longer contain $readPreferences

That was a bug, $readPreferences should only be sent to mongos.

summary: Failed authentication causes re-authentication attempt
Include PHP version in debug logs
slaveOk bit not set when using hint to query a secondary directly
description:
The following tests fail with "not master" exceptions after bumping to libmongoc 1.2.0-rc0:

 * tests/server/server-executeCommand-002.phpt
 * tests/server/server-executeQuery-005.phpt

These errors should be resolved be CDRIVER-903.

summary: Authentication error result in weird error message
SSL/TLS already set-up for this stream
BulkWrite and executeInsert() should support an options array
description:
{{executeUpdate()}} and {{executeDelete()}} currently take an options array (between the query arguments and WriteConcern); however, {{executeBulkWrite()}} and {{executeInsert()}} do not and BulkWrite's constructor only takes a boolean {{$ordered}} argument.

Current protos:

 * public function Manager::executeBulkWrite(string $namespace, BulkWrite $bulk, ?WriteConcern $writeConcern = null): WriteResult
 * public function Manager::executeInsert(string $namespace, mixed $document, ?WriteConcern $writeConcern = null): WriteResult
 * public function BulkWrite::__construct(boolean $ordered)

This leaves no way to support a "bypassDocumentValidation" option at the basic driver level. We will need to change these prototypes to allow passing of such an option (and perhaps others down the line). Since we can create a BulkWrite with options, there is no need to change {{executeBulkWrite()}}.

Proposed protos:

 * public function Manager::executeInsert(string $namespace, mixed $document, ?array $insertOptions = [], ?WriteConcern $writeConcern = null): WriteResult
 * public function BulkWrite::__construct(?array $bulkWriteOptions = [])

Actually implementation of "bypassDocumentValidation" will be handled by PHPC-443. This issue only pertains to modifying our public API to support an options array, to which we'll later add "bypassDocumentValidation".

summary: Production debug logs
description:
Currently we compile mongoc with -DMONGOC_TRACE -- which really isn't meant for production builds.

We do this though to make debugging sane.
It would be great if we can patch the mongoc tracing to be compiled in, but disabled depending on "configuration setting".

summary: toJSON and fromJSON should throw exceptions on errors
description:
{{toJSON()}} should throw UnexpectedValueExceptions (just like {{toPHP()}}):

 * Could not read document from BSON reader
 * Reading document did not exhaust input buffer

{{fromJSON()}} should throw an UnexpectedValueException on any error parsing the JSON. For now, it's suitable to simply report the {{bson_error_t}} message.

summary: Awkward php.net/mongodb link
description:
Currently http://php.net/mongodb leads to awkward TOC rather then the frontpage of the extension.

This can be easily fixed by updating https://github.com/php/web-php/blob/master/error.php

summary: Docs: Improve ecosystem landing page to include new drivers
Wrong license on PECL page
description:
The PECL page for the PHP mongodb driver

https://pecl.php.net/package/mongodb

says the license is "New BSD License", which doesn't match the LICENSE file in the download which says "Apache License Version 2.0".

summary: Dependency on openssl is added even if the extension is not marked for compilation
Fix return value comparison in check_closed stream handler
Use constant-time hash comparison functions
Manager::selectServer() should throw when failing to select a server
Use electionId to detect stale primaries.
Fix bson_init_static() precondition failure
description:
{code}
<?php                                                                                
$opts = array (                                                                      
  'allowPartialResults' => false,                                                    
  'batchSize' => 101,                                                                
  'comment' => '',                                                                   
  'cursorType' => 0,                                                                 
  'limit' => 1,                                                                      
  'maxTimeMS' => 0,                                                                  
  'modifiers' =>                                                                     
  array (                                                                            
  ),                                                                                 
  'noCursorTimeout' => false,                                                        
  'oplogReplay' => false,                                                            
  'projection' =>                                                                    
  array (                                                                            
  ),                                                                                 
  'skip' => 0,                                                                       
  'sort' =>                                                                          
  array (                                                                            
  ),                                                                                 
  'cursorFlags' => 0,                                                                
);                                                                                   
                                                                                     
$m = new MongoDB\Driver\Manager("mongodb://localhost:27017");                        
$q = new MongoDB\Driver\Query(array(), $opts);                                       
                                                                                     
$m->executeQuery("dbname.collname", $q);          
echo "I'm alive\n";
{code}

summary: Support bypassDocumentValidation option for insert and update commands
description: We need to be able to specify a "bypassDocumentValidation" option for insert and update operations. This means we need to change BulkWrite to check for this option (alongside "ordered").

summary: phongo_stream_initiator() constructs invalid stream URI for IPv6 literal
Disable async stream connections to avoid failed IPv6 connections
Typo in config.m4 prevents use of custom libsasl
description: The script iterates on {{$MONGODB_SASL}} when it should refer to {{$PHP_MONGODB_SASL}}.

summary: Default Manager URI to "mongodb://localhost:27017/"
description: The HHVM driver already does this in the interest of usability.

summary: PHP 7 build failure on 32-bit systems
description:
From PHPC-524:

summary: Cannot append reference to BSON
Symbol collision between mongodb.so and mongo.so
description: Both PHPC and the legacy driver define a {{zval_to_bson()}} implementation, which leads to symbol conflicts and sporadic crashes (see [mongodb/mongo-php-driver#173|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/issues/173]).

summary: Enable configure without SASL for UNIX builds
description: {{config.m4}} does not respect the {{--with-mongodb-sasl=no}} build option and attempts to search for a SASL directory anyway.

summary: Include read concern in Query debug output
description: This was inadvertently omitted when implementing PHPC-448.

summary: Parse readconcernlevel in URI options array
description:
According to {{mongoc_uri_parse_option()}} in {{mongoc-uri.c}}, "readconcernlevel" is only handled when processing the URI's query string. This means that PHPC may not be properly assigning the value when picking it up from Manager's {{$options}} array argument (instead of the URI string).

This should be trivial to test, given that we now have a {{Manager::getReadConcern()}} method.

summary: Suppress PHP warnings during stream init
Use ConnectionTimeoutException for server selection failures
description:
HHVM throws ConnectionTimeoutException for server selection failures, which are usually timeout errors. This is not a BC break, as we currently throw RuntimeException.

Note that we do not have a ServerSelectionTimeoutException; however, since all connection activity routes through the server selection process, it's reasonable to simply use "connection" terminology for such errors.

summary: Manager::selectServer() should select exception class based on bson_error_t
description:
Currently, {{Manager::selectServer()}} elects to always throw a RuntimeException. This is inconsistent with the other three execute methods choosing an exception class based on the {{bson_error_t}} domain/code. Combined, these are the four methods that could result in connection initialization, so they should handle exceptions in a like manner.

Note: this was not an issue before PHPC-553 and PHPC-581.

summary: Always encode ODS field when serializing Persistable documents
description:
See: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/94da5474070ad80b7e69da41c720d17a73cb94b4/src/MongoDB/BulkWrite.c#L153

This will break some of the ODS tests, which is intentional. They will need to be removed, since bsonSerialize() should not be in the business of returning atomic modifiers (at least not with Persistable objects).

summary: Update does not serialize embedded Persistable's __pclass field
Ignoring "query" param in MongoDB\Driver\Command
Encourage users to handle _id field in Persistable documentation
description:
This also applies to the BSON Serializable and Unserializable interfaces.

Additionally, we should discourage users from returning atomic modifiers from {{Persistable::bsonSerialize()}} (see: PHPC-550).

See: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/issues/104#issuecomment-174719283

summary: Consult SIZEOF_ZEND_LONG for 64-bit integer support
Property name corrupted when unserializing 64-bit integer on 32-bit platform
Throw exception if Binary subtype is out of range
description:
The [BSON specification|http://bsonspec.org/spec.html] defines a binary subtype as one byte. Since PHP can only parse it as an integer, we should throw an exception if the argument is out of range (i.e. less than zero or greater than 255).

Currently, a larger integer will be silently truncated when assigned to an {{uint8_t}} in {{bson_append_binary()}}.

summary: Throw exception if Timestamp arguments are out of range
description: The [BSON specification|http://bsonspec.org/spec.html] defines a timestamp's components as two 32-bit integer values (timestamp and increment). If a 64-bit integer is passed on an allowing platform, the values could be silently truncated to {{uint32_t}} types when calling {{bson_append_timestamp()}}. We should throw an exception if the arguments exceed the range of 32-bit integers.

summary: Throw exception if WriteConcern wtimeout is out of range
description:
libmongoc limits wtimeout to a signed, 32-bit integer.

We already throw an exception if wtimeout is negative, but we should also throw if the value is > INT32_MAX; otherwise, input might be silently truncated.

summary: Use more descriptive messages in WriteExceptions
Segfault due to double free by corrupt BSON visitor
toJSON() should throw on invalid BSON
description:
Currently, {{MongoDB\BSON\toJSON()}} only throws if we cannot read exactly one document from the input stream.

Corrupt BSON (e.g. flipping a field's type  to the wrong byte) causes {{bson_as_json()}} to return NULL, which we simply return as an empty string. Instead, we should check for NULL and raise an exception.

summary: toJSON() should return after throwing for bson_reader_read() failure
description: If {{bson_reader_read()}} fails, an exception is thrown; however, we still make an additional call to check for EOF, which may itself throw a separate exception (capturing the original under {{getPrevious()}}). We should return after the first exception is thrown.

summary: Document and array visitors should throw for corrupt BSON
description: Currently, the document and array visitors ignore corrupt BSON and leave their zval initialized and unmodified. Instead, they should signal to their calling context that they've encountered an error and further iteration should stop.

summary: ObjectID only supports lower case hexadecimal letters
description: See: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/issues/253

summary: Numeric keys limited to unsigned 32-bit integer
FPM segfaults during shutdown
description:
PHP Bugreport: https://bugs.php.net/bug.php?id=71821

MongoDB extension causes zend_mm_heap corrupted

$ sudo /usr/sbin/php-fpm7.0 -t; php -m |grep mongo
[15-Mar-2016 11:15:50] NOTICE: configuration file /etc/php/7.0/fpm/php-fpm.conf test is successful

zend_mm_heap corrupted
22:mongodb

summary: Use case insensitive parsing for Manager connectTimeoutMS array option
description: libmongoc uses case-insensitive parsing for options in the URI string, as does {{php_phongo_make_uri()}} for options in Manager's second array argument; however, {{phongo_stream_initiator()}} was using case-sensitive {{bson_iter_init_find()}} to retrieve the "connectTimeoutMS" option to apply to the PHP stream. We should either use {{bson_iter_init_find_case()}} or {{mongoc_uri_get_option_as_int32()}} instead.

summary: BulkWrite::insert() does not generate ObjectID if another field has "_id" prefix
description: When {{phongo_zval_to_bson()}} checks whether an "_id" field should be injected (for insertions), it uses {{strncmp()}} and limits the comparison to three characters. This means it will skip "_id" injection if another field has an "_id" prefix, which ultimately puts the onus the server to generate an ID for the document.

summary: ObjectID constructor should not modify string argument's memory
description: The lowercase conversion introduced in PHPC-620 modifies the input argument's memory, which may leave it modified after the constructor returns.

summary: Duplicate key error when executing a BulkWrite with insertions multiple times
Document MongoDB\Driver\Server type constants
description:
The PHP documentation for MongoDB\Driver\Server shows five TYPE_* constants being defined by the class: https://secure.php.net/manual/en/class.mongodb-driver-server.php

The documentation is incorrect as using reflection one can see the following constants:
{code}
php > var_dump((new ReflectionClass('MongoDB\Driver\Server'))->getConstants());
array(9) {
  'TYPE_UNKNOWN' =>
  int(0)
  'TYPE_STANDALONE' =>
  int(1)
  'TYPE_MONGOS' =>
  int(2)
  'TYPE_POSSIBLE_PRIMARY' =>
  int(3)
  'TYPE_RS_PRIMARY' =>
  int(4)
  'TYPE_RS_SECONDARY' =>
  int(5)
  'TYPE_RS_ARBITER' =>
  int(6)
  'TYPE_RS_OTHER' =>
  int(7)
  'TYPE_RS_GHOST' =>
  int(8)
}
{code}

summary: All BSON doc links broken
description:
All existing links to the BSON docs are broken after http://svn.php.net/viewvc?view=revision&revision=337902

A redirect must be setup for them.

summary: Rephrase unsupported/corrupt BSON messages
Keep mongo_ssl_opt_t.pem_file valid for life of mongoc_client_t
Report bypassDocumentValidation in BulkWrite debug output
Unacknowledged WriteResults have null counts
description:
Starting with 1.4.0-dev, libmongoc now omits the count fields from the write result BSON for unacknowledged writes. This causes our methods to return {{null}}, while they previously returned zero.

I propose we allow this behavior change and document it as such in PHPC-653. {{getModifiedCount}} already had behavior to conditionally return null, since it is omitted from acknowledged writes to older servers. This would extend that behavior of "null if unavailable" to the other count fields.

Note: The getter methods in PHPLIB already throw for unacknowledged writes (PHPLIB-68).

summary: Remind users that connection URIs should be encoded
description: Per the [connection string specification|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/connection-string/connection-string-spec.rst], URI components should be encoded to facilitate proper parsing. This may differ from the legacy driver, which did not fully implement the specification.

summary: Check HAVE_OPENSSL_EXT before calling php-ssl.c functions
description:
If the extension is compiled against a version of PHP without OpenSSL, the functions in {{src/contrib/php-ssl.h}} will not be declared (they are wrapped in an {{#ifdef HAVE_OPENSSL_EXT}} block); however, {{php_phongo_ssl_verify()}} still attempts to call these functions, which results in missing symbol errors.

We should check {{#ifdef HAVE_OPENSSL_EXT}} again from {{php_phongo_ssl_verify()}} and report an error to the user:

bq. Cannot verify certificate expiration. Please ensure that extension is compiled against PHP with OpenSSL or disable the "verify_expiry" SSL context option.

{{php_phongo_peer_verify()}} also needs a similar check for the built-in host name verification on PHP versions before 5.6:

bq. Cannot verify remote certificate SubjectAltName or CN. Please ensure that extension is compiled against PHP with OpenSSL or disable the "verify_peer_name" SSL context option.

summary: Rename "php_mongo" functions to not conflict with legacy driver
description:
The functions in {{src/contrib/php-ssl.c}} and {{php_mongo_verify_hostname()}} in {{php_phongo.c}} currently have a "php_mongo" prefix, which means their names may conflict with those from the legacy driver if both extensions are loaded.

This likely does not cause an issue in practice, since the functions are identical (since being imported in [this commit|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/1ab97b3bc32171b5ecb60a248da3085219dc7ca2]); however, we should still prefix them correctly.

summary: Cursor::isDead() returns true despite cursor being alive
description:
Since upgrading libmongoc to 1.4.0-dev (PHPC-629), the following tests have been failing:

 * tests/cursor/cursor-destruct-001.phpt
 * tests/cursor/cursor-isDead-001.phpt

See [this Travis CI build|https://travis-ci.org/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/jobs/123097571#L1415] for details. This may be an upstream regression in {{mongoc_cursor_is_alive()}}, but further investigation is required.

summary: Avoid mongoc_client_t use-after-free by Cursor and Server
description:
Steps to reproduce:

 * Populate a collection with a number of documents.
 * Execute a query with a limit that would return less than the number of matched documents without said limit.
 * Iterate on the cursor and allow it to be freed during standard garbage collection

A segfault can be reproduced easily via PHP 5.x's built-in web server (but not PHP 7) and querying via the legacy OP_QUERY code path (i.e. MongoDB <3.2). There appears to be a race condition between freeing the cursor and the client. If the client is freed first, the underlying stream is destroyed. When the cursor is later freed, libmongoc attempts to kill the cursor before freeing it, which results in an attempt to use a null stream pointer.

Relevant backtrace info:

In this case, we see the BulkWrite and its returned WriteResult object being freed. Freeing of the Manager object begins with the {{mongoc_topology_description_destroy()}} block (topologies are freed for single-threaded drivers). As part of this freeing, {{mongoc_stream_destroy()}} is called (while freeing the underlying topology scanner and each connected node). The {{mongoc_cluster_destroy()}} block is the last evidence of the Manager's free sequence. After this point, the Cursor is freed and we encountered a segfault while selecting a stream for the server, which is now null.

summary: UTCDateTime::toDateTime() does not properly set microseconds
Do not persist SSL streams to avoid SSL reinitialization errors
description:
This is a quick solution to address the issue described in [mongodb/mongo-php-driver#328|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/issues/328], where the extension attempts to re-initialize SSL on a persistent stream and throws.

Note that the current socket hash only includes the host, port, and URI and may omit SSL connection options. Since it is possible for SSL options to change in subsequent Manager constructions, it is safer to avoid persisting the connection altogether. A work-around where we either allowed SSL reinitialize to fail or avoided it altogether (if it was previously performed on the socket) might mean that some SSL options could be ignored for the current construction.

A more robust solution will be implemented in PHPC-605, when we begin persisting the entire libmongoc client structure and incorporate all of the Manager construction options (including all SSL options) into its hash.

description:
" Connecting to MongoDB with SSL and a certificate: " on http://php.net/manual/en/mongodb-driver-manager.construct.php is both wrong and uses a third party service as hostnames.


The example password in the previous connection string also feels very inappropriate saying "Hell people"

summary: Rename "javascript" to "code" in Javascript BSON class
description: The BSON spec and forthcoming getter method (PHPC-730) refer to "code", so we should use that term for the internal property as well. This has implications for {{var_dump()}}, {{var_export()}}, and serialization.

summary: Use consistent exceptions for BSON initialization methods
description:
As of PHPC-460, the {{__construct()}}, {{__set_state()}}, and {{__wakeup()}} methods in BSON classes use the same init functions. Those init functions should be responsible for throwing exceptions (they currently just return true/false) to ensure consistent error reporting for each method of instantiating a BSON object.

This is not a concern for {{MongoDB\BSON\toPHP()}}, as they would already throw for invalid BSON data.

summary: Javascript constructor should throw if code contains null bytes
description: Although http://bsonspec.org/spec.html might seem to allow this, libbson does not permit null bytes in Javascript code strings.

summary: Regex constructor should throw if pattern or flags contain null bytes
description: BSON's regex type uses null-terminated strings for pattern and flag components. Since values would be truncated by libbson, which uses {{strlen()}} internally, we should throw if either argument contains a null byte.

summary: Default Manager URI to "mongodb://127.0.0.1/"
description:
We originally defaulted to {{"mongodb://localhost:27017/"}} in PHPC-509 (released in 1.1.0), but this was removed due to refactoring in PHPC-368 (released in 1.1.1). Since then, we rely on libmongoc's default of {{"mongodb://127.0.0.1/"}}. Let's explicitly apply that default in the driver and update our documentation.

When the behavior was originally changed in PHPC-368, there might have been implications for preferring IPv4 connections by default instead of resolving localhost to a possible IPv6 address (see: PHPC-512).

summary: CursorID is not properly expressed on 32-bit platforms
description:
{{Cursor::\_\_toString()}} uses [{{RETVAL_LONG()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/1ac174659febb98660807e29b7c9832b277e50e4/src/MongoDB/CursorId.c#L63] to convert the cursor ID to a string. On a 32-bit platform, the uint64_t cannot be fully represented in the zval, which makes this an inaccurate conversion. We should use the same logic found in {{UTCDateTime::\_\_toString()}} (i.e. {{sprintf()}} with an type-appropriate pattern).

Similarly, {{var_dump()}} needs to print the cursor ID as a string on 32-bit platforms. It currently uses an integer on all platforms.

summary: Remove libmongoc-priv system dependency in config.m4
description:
As of 1.4.0, the libmongoc-priv package is removed. As of PHPC-542, PHPC no longer relies on its symbols, so the check should be changed.

This only applies when libmongoc is used as a system library.

summary: Remove include of private mongoc-trace.h
description: In the newest version of the C Driver, mongoc.h will include mongoc-trace.h, and including mongoc-trace.h (since its a private header) directly will give an error. The php driver directly includes mongoc-trace.h from php_phongo.c.

summary: config.w32 should define MONGOC_ENABLE_SASL
description: AFAICT, {{config.w32}} is always defining {{HAVE_MONGODB_SASL=1}}, which isn't referenced elsewhere in the extension or libmongoc. Based on {{config.m4}} and libmongoc's own conditional, we should actually be setting {{MONGOC_ENABLE_SASL}}.

summary: Windows builds should generate libmongoc and libbson config and version files
description:
Following instructions for  [compiling PECL extensions on Windows|https://wiki.php.net/internals/windows/stepbystepbuild#building_pecl_extensions], I noticed that the version files for libmongoc and libbson (e.g. {{bson-version.h}}) were not regenerated. This causes PHPC to report an outdated version for both bundled libraries.

For instances, a build of {{1.0.0-beta2}} reported libbson 1.1.3, even though the submodule is clearly 1.2.0-rc0.

summary: Windows reports wrong mongoc and libbson versions
undefined symbol: mongoc_write_concern_is_acknowledged

summary: Improve error messages for invalid Decimal128 and ObjectID strings
description:
As mentioned in [PR #346|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/346], PHPC and HHVM differ in their Decimal128 exception message for an invalid string. For the ObjectID message, both drivers are consistent, but the error message is brief and does not report the invalid value.

I propose we change these messages to the following:

 * Error parsing Decimal128 string: %s
 * Error parsing ObjectID string: %s

HHVM currently refers to "The argument", which I'd like to remove, since this error will be common to {{\_\_construct()}}, {{\_\_set_state()}}, and {{\_\_wakeup()}}. It is merely a field instead of a proper method argument for the last two functions.

summary: Regex constructor should default flags arg to empty string
description:
This seems like a usability improvement (similar to PHPC-536 for UTCDateTime).

The flags field can be left as required for {{\_\_wakeup()}} and {{\_\_set_state()}}, since it will always be present in {{serialize()}} and {{var_export()}} output.

summary: Allow cross-platform serialization of Timestamp and UTCDateTime
description:
Opening this as a tracking ticket. We can debate whether it is worth implementing down the line.

With PHPC-460, our BSON types are serializing the same properties reported by {{var_dump()}}, since we use the same {{get_properties}} handler. Since both Timestamp components are unsigned 32-bit integers, a 64-bit PHP system might report a positive integer (e.g. 4294967295) that cannot be unserialized on a 32-bit platform.

Since UTCDateTime is a 64-bit integer, we'd likely serialize it as a string on 32-bit platforms and an integer on 64-bit platforms.

summary: Parse Timestamp argument as strings to accept large integers
description:
Timestamp's increment and timestamp components are unsigned 32-bit integers (max: 4294967295). On a 32-bit platform, 2147483647 will be the largest value accepted by the constructor since PHP's integers are signed.

It's possible for such values to exist on a 32-bit platform after unserializing a Timestamp from raw BSON, in which case it would be printed as a negative value (via {{ZVAL_LONG}}).

On a 32-bit platform, we should accept strings for these values, as we already do for UTCDateTime's milliseconds (a signed 64-bit integer). There are no Timestamp getter methods, so we need not worry about their return values. Strings can also be returned on 32-bit for {{var_dump()}}, serialization, and {{var_export()}} purposes (PHPC-460).

summary: Persist topology state between requests
description:
While working on multi-request testing for phongo I realized phongo hasn't been updated to deal with the mongoc-1.2 SDAM changes.

We correctly keep the stream alive between requests, but upon returning the existing stream on the next request, mongoc will still do a full SDAM demanded discovery - which includes the whole ismaster shebang routines.

This means on every PHP request we issue ismaster call to every node as-if we were creating a new connection.

summary: Validate that read preference tag set is an array of documents
description: If the read preference tags are malformed (i.e. not an empty array or array of objects with scalar key/value pairs) we should raise an InvalidArgumentException.

description: See: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/130/files#r43516997

summary: Do not unnecessarily wrap filters in $query
Ensure read preference tags sets serialize as documents
description: An empty tag set in the Manager options or ReadPreference constructor currently serializes as an empty PHP array. We should ensure that this is serialized as an empty BSON document (not an array) in the read preference structure.

summary: Empty ReadConcern and WriteConcern should serialize to BSON documents
description:
Since ReadConcern and WriteConcern may be empty by default, it's possible for their {{bsonSerialize()}} methods to return an empty array. This will result in serialization to a BSON array, which would be rejected by the server. We should ensure an object is always returned from these methods.

This is not an issue for ReadPreference, since it will always contain at least a "mode" field; however, we can also return an object for good measure.

description: The install docs on http://php.net/manual/en/mongodb.setup.php don't say anything about required dependencies, such as cyrus-sasl or openssl, or how to install them on different OS.

description: As per https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/issues/299#issuecomment-210468279

summary: Document that persistent sockets should not be re-used after forking
description:
Forked processes are prone to re-using a previous socket, which can lead to all sorts of IO errors (e.g. out-of-order replies on the wire protocol) between the parent and child processes.

The legacy driver annotated persisted sockets with a PID so that they could be forgotten during selection if the PID did not still match.

Per PHPC-433, we should include the process ID in the mongoc_client_t hash to ensure that persisted clients are not reused if a forked process constructs a new Manager. We should also document that Managers should not be created before forking and used in child processes.

summary: fromJSON() should not evaluate bson_error_t.message as boolean
description:
In {{bson.c}}, {{fromJson}} calls {{phongo_throw_exception}} with either a custom error message, or a generic "Error parsing JSON" message.

To determine whether or not to use a custom error message (bubbled up from mongoc) it checks whether a {{bson_error_t}}'s {{message}} field is NULL. Since the {{message}} field is just an array, it is never NULL. This produces a warning on some compilers.

To check if the {{bson_error_t}} has an error message, {{error.code}} or {{error.domain}} should be checked instead.

summary: Do not allow BulkWrite objects to be executed multiple times
description: Although libmongoc allows a {{mongoc_bulk_operation_t}} to be executed multiple times, it does not make sense to do so. It is also prone to duplicate key errors, since we generate {{_id}} values when documents are scheduled for insertion (see: PHPC-360).

summary: Replace PHONGO_API with exported get_x_class_entry functions
description: Currently, we are using the {{PHONGO_API}} macro to export our class definitions. Instead we should provide {{get_x_class_entry()}} exported functions (using {{PHPAPI}}), as is done for the DateTime component.

summary: UTCDateTime constructor should truncate floats
description:
PHPC-726 changed the Timestamp and UTCDateTime initialization methods to convert strings to integers and raise exceptions on failure. This process is more strict that PHP's own string-to-integer conversion, as the following fails:

{code}new MongoDB\BSON\UTCDatetime(microtime(true) * 1000);{code}

bq. MongoDB\Driver\Exception\InvalidArgumentException with message 'Error parsing "1473959591217.2" as 64-bit integer for MongoDB\BSON\UTCDateTime initialization'

We should address this before it affects many users that were constructing UTCDateTime for the current time using {{microtime()}}, which was necessary before we started defaulting to the current time in 1.2.0-alpha1 (PHPC-536).

Note: There is no practical reason to support truncation of floats for Timestamp arguments.

summary: Timestamp and UTCDateTime constructors should accept integers in strict types mode
description:
The final {{zend_parse_parameters()}} calls in these functions (respecting the DateTimeInterface allowance for UTCDateTime) uses the "s" type. This causes PHP 7 to throw a TypeError if a string is not passed.

Since [union types|https://wiki.php.net/rfc/union_types] are not available, we cannot hint for {{int|string}} and need some other way to relax the error and allow integers to be converted to strings.

For context, the constructor argument parsing was last changed in [d25e565|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/d25e565f647d824ced02146bd41d8ef17ff93cb9] for PHPC-726.

summary: Javascript objects are always serialized as BSON code types without scope
description:
This bug dates back to the very first release and was introduced in [dfbef38|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/dfbef380a579e2d0eba236c0faa53e559d380578] for PHPC-74.

Javascript objects with a scope are erroneously serialized as BSON code types without a scope. Javascript objects without a scope are erroneously serialized as BSON code_with_scope types; however, since their scope pointer is null, {{bson_append_code_with_scope()}} defensively falls back to {{bson_append_code()}}.

This was discovered while updating the PHPC-459 tests for libmongoc-1.5.0-rc6, which includes CDRIVER-1335.

summary: Implement comparison handlers for BSON types
Fix BSON encoding of immutable arrays and documents with circular references
description:
When a numeric array was encoded to BSON, the hash's apply counter was incremented and decremented irrespective of whether apply protection was enabled. This was not compatible with immutable arrays, which may be created by OPcache. We should consult {{ZEND_HASH_APPLY_PROTECTION()}} whenever an apply counter might be modified.

Previously, {{object_to_bson()}} incremented the apply counter when converting a {{MongoDB\BSON\Type}} instance. Associative arrays and other objects were not protected from recursion and could segfault when being encoded if they contained circular references.

summary: config.w32 fails to generate all libmongoc and libbson version constants
description:
Two problems to fix:

 * {{mongodb_parse_version_file()}} needs to detect if the pre-release string is empty. It currently replaces the "PRERELEASE_VERSION" constants with "undefined" if it is empty.
 * {{mongodb_generate_header()}} must do global replacements. Since the version file uses multiple replacements for a single replacement string, the current logic is prone to leaving replacements beyond the first unchanged.

summary: Do not depend on date and standard extensions in config.w32
Cursor::setTypeMap() leaks current element if called during iteration
Client persistence crashes with ZTS (e.g. Apache)
Segfault when ReadPreference tag sets are an immutable array

summary: High number of persisted server connections with Apache
description:
mongodb连接太多，并且无法自动关闭

我用的时apache的event模式做php开发，发现每次查询mongodb数据，连接数就增加一个，很快就有几百个连接了，并且一直不关闭，这样下去我的服务器资源就要被这些进程堵死了

summary: Running a command against a server that no connection can be made against, shows weird exception message
Unexpected connection error calling ismaster
description:
From 1000 calls to MongoDB we are getting 30 failures. This is the exception that we're getting:
{quote}
No suitable servers found (`serverselectiontryonce` set): [connection error calling ismaster on
{quote}

We are using 
* PHP version: php 7.0
* Mongo DB driver: php7.0-mongodb, 1.1.8-1~dotdeb+8.1, amd64
* OS: Debian 8, x86_64
* MongoDB shell version: 3.2.8

Is this issue in you attention?
Is there a workaround at this point?

Thank you,
Andrei

summary: Unable to insert document in mongoDB from PHP
description: I want to install php driver. I run the command "composer require "mongodb/mongodb=^1.0.0" with phpDriver "php_mongodb-1.1.8-5.6-ts-vc11-x64" from "http://pecl.php.net/package/mongodb" and add extension in php.ini in php5.6 as well as apache's php.ini. By calling phpinfo(), it is showing installed mongoDriver. But when I call it by  "new MongoDB();" it gives the error "class not found" I tried with "new MongoDB\Client("mongodb://localhost:27017");" but on calling the insert function for adding document. it gives the error that inset function does not exist. Any way of going out?

summary: Do not use system crypto policy for OpenSSL on Windows
description: {{MONGOC_ENABLE_CRYPTO_SYSTEM_PROFILE}} should not be used for our Windows builds. The functionality is primarily intended for Linux distributions with custom profiles (e.g. Fedora). [d227207|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/d227207f970e590c3149979a675b17a0362145fa] for PHPC-703 incorrectly assumed that libmongoc was enabling it by default on Windows.

summary: Do not allow mixing bundled and system libbson and libmongoc libraries
description:
Quoting my comment from PHPC-856:

bq. Also, I would introduce some logic to not allow mixing bundled and system libraries. If libbson is bundled, only the PHP extension is going to use that code, while the libmongoc system library will use its own version of libbson. That could lead to odd behavior down the line.

summary: WriteResult debug handler should return objects
description: Currently, WriteResult's debug handler flattens its internal objects (e.g. upserted IDs, write errors) to arrays. We should expose them as objects, which will allow us to re-use code from the getters and also make {{__debugInfo()}} more usable when we implement PHPC-470.

summary: Assertion failure in stream_not_found() after failed getmore operation
description:
PHPC has two tests for throwing an exception on a failed getmore. These tests spin up their own server with mongo-orchestration and configure the failReceivedGetmore fail point.

After upgrading from 1.5.0-rc3 to 1.5.0-rc4, both of these tests trigger an assert failure in libmongoc:

{noformat}
phpc/src/libmongoc/src/mongoc/mongoc-cluster.c:1470 stream_not_found(): precondition failed: error
Aborted (core dumped)

Termsig=6
{noformat}

Based on the libmongoc trace log, this assert failure happens when the {{mongoc_client_t}} is destroyed during process shutdown. Our trace logs end with:

{noformat}
OK: Got MongoDB\Driver\Exception\ConnectionException
